aliases: 椰奶咖椰酱 (Yē Nǎi Kāyē Jiàng)

chinese: 椰奶咖椰酱 (Yē Nǎi Kāyē Jiàng)

food_subgroup: Egg-coconut milk sugar jam spread; traditional Southeast Asian breakfast condiment

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Pandan Kaya specifically. Closest parent would be a generic egg-based sweet spread; no exact or close FoodOn term identified as of 2026.
ontology.foodex2_description: Coconut egg jam spread (closest parent match; no exact FoodEx2 code for pandan kaya)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0GHS used as closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor for a coconut-egg-sugar jam. No exact FoodEx2 code for pandan kaya exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for pandan kaya or coconut egg jam.

serving.description: 2 tablespoons (~30g) — standard application on 2 slices of kaya toast
serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of pandan kaya is approximately 2 tablespoons (30g), spread across 2 slices of toast as part of a traditional Singaporean kaya toast breakfast set.

nutrient_source.title: Pandan Kaya — HPB Singapore (Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Pandan Kaya. HPB Singapore is the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes. Values are accepted as verified.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of sucrose-based egg-coconut jam — analogous estimate based on sucrose GI moderated by egg protein and coconut fat matrix
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (analogous estimate basis)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for pandan kaya exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI ~60 is estimated from sucrose GI (~65) moderated downward by the egg protein and coconut fat matrix, which slows gastric emptying and glucose absorption. GL per 30g serving = (60 × 14g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 8.4. Confidence 0.9. This estimation methodology is consistent with University of Sydney GI Database analogous-food estimation principles.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (50mg per 100g; ~15mg per 30g serving), but high saturated fat (8.3g per 100g) and high sugar (46.7g per 100g) present long-term cardiovascular risk
hypertension.guidance: Pandan kaya is low in sodium (~15mg per 30g serving) and is acceptable for hypertension management in isolation, but the high saturated fat and sugar content warrant portion control, particularly when consumed as part of the full kaya toast set breakfast which contributes ~600mg sodium from bread, butter, and eggs combined.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (46.7g per 100g; ~14g per 30g serving) with medium GI (~60) and moderate GL (~8.4 per serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Pandan kaya has a medium GI (~60) and a moderate glycemic load (~8.4 per 30g serving); individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to one tablespoon (~15g, GL ~4.2) and account for the full kaya toast breakfast GL (~26–28) when managing postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat from coconut milk (8.3g per 100g; ~2.5g per 30g serving) combined with high sugar (46.7g per 100g) and dietary cholesterol from eggs (70mg per 100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Pandan kaya contributes ~2.5g saturated fat and ~21mg cholesterol per 30g serving; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake to one tablespoon per occasion and monitor total saturated fat intake across the full kaya toast set meal, which may contribute 8–12g saturated fat when butter is included.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pandan Kaya is Singapore's iconic coconut egg jam — a glossy, aromatic spread made by slowly cooking eggs, coconut milk, sugar, and pandan leaf extract together until thick and custard-like. It is the defining condiment of the Singaporean kaya toast breakfast set, served at kopitiams (traditional coffee shops) such as Ya Kun Kaya Toast and Killiney Kopitiam. The spread has a rich, creamy texture with a distinctive green colour from pandan and a fragrant, vanilla-like aroma. Per 100g: 317 kcal, 5g protein, 13.3g fat (8.3g saturated from coconut milk), 46.7g carbohydrate (all sugar), 0.5g dietary fibre, 50mg sodium. Per standard 30g serving (2 tablespoons): ~95 kcal, 1.5g protein, 4g fat (~2.5g saturated), 14g sugar, GI ~60, GL ~8.4, ~15mg sodium. The full kaya toast set (2 slices toast + 10g butter + 30g kaya + 2 soft-boiled eggs + kopi/teh) provides approximately 460 kcal, 20g protein, 22g fat, 44g carbohydrate, and ~600mg sodium. Pandan antioxidant claims are negligible at culinary doses — pandan functions as flavour and colour only. Kaya means 'rich' in Malay, reflecting the richness of this egg-coconut preparation. Source: HPB Singapore (Foodo). Confidence: high.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore (Foodo), the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes. All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and internally consistent. GI is an analogous estimate with documented methodology, confidence 0.9. Ontology uses a closest-parent FoodEx2 anchor with honest gap notes.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for pandan kaya not separately reported in HPB Singapore Foodo database; only total fat and saturated fat are available. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in HPB Singapore Foodo database for this item. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per HPB Singapore source (no hydrogenated fats in traditional kaya preparation). All core macros (protein_g 5.0, fat_g 13.3, carbohydrate_g 46.7) are non-zero and fully supported. GI ~60 is an analogous estimate based on sucrose GI moderated by egg protein and coconut fat matrix; no direct GI measurement for pandan kaya exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GL per 30g serving = (60 × 14g) / 100 = 8.4. Two default portions are defined: 1 tablespoon = 20g and 2 tablespoons = 30g (standard kaya toast application). Pandan antioxidant marketing claims are not supported at culinary doses and are not reflected in the nutrient profile.
